oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_bridge_payload_decoding.json.description: Decode bridge message JSON payloads into a requested structured payload shape, returning the decoded fields or a null data marker when the payload cannot satisfy that shape.

feature2_bridge_message_replacement.json.description: Create an outbound bridge message by preserving the incoming identifier, component, and metadata while replacing the event and either a raw JSON payload or an encoded structured payload.

feature3_bridge_payload_encoding_errors..description: Report language-neutral error categories when a structured bridge payload cannot be encoded because JSON conversion support is missing or invalid.

feature4_visit_response_summary.json.description: Render a visit response summary with status, normalized HTML preview, middle truncation for long responses, and original response length.

input.response_html: <html><head></head>This is a really long response that is truncated.</html>

cases.expected_output:
status=200
response_html=<html><head></head>This i [...] that is truncated.</html>
response_length=75

input.response_html:
<html>
<head></head>This   is a really long response that is truncated.
</html>

cases.expected_output:
status=200
response_html=<html><head></head>This i [...] that is truncated.</html>
response_length=79

feature5_http_status_classification.json.description: Classify HTTP response status codes into client, server, or unknown error categories while preserving the numeric status and known reason phrase.

cases.expected_output:
status=505
category=server
reason=Http Version Not Supported

feature6_web_load_error_classification.j.description: Classify browser page-load error codes into stable categories and descriptions, using an other category for unrecognized codes.

feature7_ssl_certificate_error_classific.description: Classify SSL certificate validation error codes into stable certificate-error categories and descriptions, using an other category for unrecognized codes.

feature8_path_configuration_lookup.json.description: Load navigation path configuration and return the merged properties that match each requested URL path, including settings, context, presentation, title, refresh behavior, and destination URI.

cases.expected_output:
rules_count=10
settings_count=1
setting_custom_app_feature_enabled=true
url=https://turbo.hotwired.dev/home
context=default
presentation=clear_all
query_string_presentation=default
title=
pull_to_refresh_enabled=true
uri=hotwire://fragment/web/home
url=https://turbo.hotwired.dev/new
context=modal
presentation=default
query_string_presentation=default
title=
pull_to_refresh_enabled=false
uri=hotwire://fragment/web/modal
url=https://turbo.hotwired.dev/edit
context=modal
presentation=default
query_string_presentation=default
title=
pull_to_refresh_enabled=false
uri=hotwire://fragment/web/modal
url=https://turbo.hotwired.dev/image.jpg
context=default
presentation=default
query_string_presentation=default
title=Image Viewer
pull_to_refresh_enabled=true
uri=hotwire://fragment/image_viewer

feature9_navigation_route_classification.description: Classify a requested URL against the application start host for in-app navigation or external handoff routes, preserving the route decision and match result.

_constraints.input_condition: action == "decode_bridge_payload" and payload_schema matches structure defined in message.json_data
_constraints.output_rule: Output key=value pairs on separate lines. The keys must match the property names in the decoded payload shapes (specifically 'title' and 'subtitle')

_constraints.input_condition: action == "decode_bridge_payload" AND payload_schema does NOT match message.json_data structure
_constraints.output_rule: Output null marker and newline.
Output key=value pairs. The 'json_data' field value must be the raw JSON string provided in 'replacement_json'. Category, Event, and Metadata URL are updated; Id is preserved.
Output key=value pairs. The 'json_data' field value is the JSON-serialized form of the replacement_data object.
Priority of keys in output: id, component, event, metadata_url, json_data. All lines must end with a newline.
Output error category message.
Output error category message.
Output status, null marker for html, and length 0.

_constraints.input_condition: action == "summarize_visit_response" and response_html is not null
_constraints.output_rule: Normalize HTML: Collapsed whitespace, explicit newlines collapsed. Truncate middle with '...' if long. Output status, truncated html string, and original length.
Output format must be three lines: status=..., response_html=..., response_length=... Each line ending with newline.
category=success. Output includes reason phrase from standard list or blank.
category=client. Output includes standard reason phrase (e.g., 'Bad Request')
category=server. Output includes standard reason phrase (e.g., 'Internal Server Error')

category=unknown. Reason phrase may be blank or standard for known codes outside 4xx/5xx if applicable, but classification rule is unknown for the primary categorization logic as implied by 'unknown' category for out of range.
Output order: status=..., category=..., reason=... Each line ending with newline.

_constraints.input_condition: action == "classify_web_load_error" and error_code matches negative specific values (-1 to -12)
_constraints.output_rule: Map error_code to specific category and description. Exact mapping required for each code.

_constraints.input_condition: action == "classify_web_load_error" and error_code is -22 (-21 is unknown)
_constraints.output_rule: category=unknown, description=Unknown

_constraints.input_condition: any other unrecognized error_code (positive or negative not in list)
_constraints.output_rule: category=unknown, description=Unknown
Map error_code to specific category and description.
category=other. description is blank.

_constraints.input_condition: any other unrecognized error_code (positive or negative not in list)
_constraints.output_rule: category=other, description=blank

Output rules_count, count of settings, and properties for each URL in a specific fixed order. Properties must include: context, presentation, query_string_presentation, title, pull_to_refresh_enabled, uri, url, setting_custom_app_feature_enabled.
decision=navigate, matches=true
decision=navigate, matches=false
decision=cancel, if external hostname then matches=true, else matches=false
decision=cancel, if external hostname then matches=true, else matches=false
Output order: route_kind, start_host, target_host, decision, matches. Each line ending with newline.
